rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference visible ports, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool for constants and equations.
Ensure type consistency between assumed types and actual data types in expressions.
Only include concrete bounds or named constants in assumptions and guarantees when provided.
Avoid unsafe Boolean-to-integer conversions; use exact Boolean expressions when port types are bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Constants and equations must use valid AGREE type names (int, real, bool).
Guarantees should express output behavior in terms of input or assumed state.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through visible variables.
Do not generate range assumptions or guarantees without concrete bounds or named constants.
